channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: China’s Xi warns Trump about Taiwan at Beijing summit
item.description: The Chinese leader cautions the US president that disagreement over Taiwan could send relations down a dangerous path.

item.title: UK Health Secretary Wes Streeting resigns from government
item.description: In a statement, Streeting said that he no longer had 'confidence' in Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s leadership.

item.title: Trump and Xi hail ‘most important’ relationship
item.description: US President Donald Trump described his country’s relationship with China as ‘one of the most consequential’.

item.title: Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano Netflix MMA fight: Card, start, how to watch
item.description: Trailblazing mixed martial arts fighters end lengthy retirements for highly-anticipated bout to be shown on Netflix.

item.title: Labour’s Angela Rayner says she has been cleared over UK tax affairs
item.description: Rayner says she has been cleared by HMRC, paving the way for a potential Labour leadership challenge.

item.title: Two killed as Israel ramps up southern Lebanon attacks ahead of US talks
item.description: Lebanon and Israel are expected to hold talks on extending the ceasefire in Washington.

item.title: Zimbabweans lured into Russia’s war as families plead for their return
item.description: Families call on governments in Moscow and Harare to secure the return of loved ones from Ukrainian front lines.

item.title: Who are the US CEOs in China with Trump, and what’s in it for them?
item.description: Top executives hope to expand their businesses in China as Trump aims for win ahead of midterm vote.

item.title: Russia launches hundreds more drones at Ukraine, killing three people
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Russia fired 'more than 1,560 drones' at Ukraine since Wednesday.

item.title: World Cup train and shuttle bus ticket prices cut in New York, New Jersey
item.description: Round-trip train tickets brought down to $98 from $150, and bus fares to cost $20 instead of $80, state officials say.

item.title: Could Iran war trigger a hunger crisis?
item.description: The UN warns disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz could drive up food and fertiliser costs, and worsen global hunger.

item.title: Federal judge blocks US sanctions against UN rapporteur Francesca Albanese
item.description: US sanctions imposed on UN expert Francesca Albanese by the Trump administration temporarily ⁠blocked by a judge.

item.title: In the UK, Muslim votes are treated as a problem to be managed
item.description: The UK's local elections exposed the suspicion that meets Muslim political participation.

item.title: Lionel Messi scores two goals as Inter Miami rally to beat Cincinnati
item.description: Argentinian star forward's brace and an assist steer Miami to their fifth consecutive away victory in the MLS in the US.

item.title: Japanese snacks go black-and-white: Why Iran war is driving up ink prices
item.description: Calbee says it would temporarily use only black and white colours on 14 of its products due to a lack of printing ink.

item.title: Trump praises China as he ignores question about Taiwan
item.description: Trump praises China as he ignores question about Taiwan

item.title: UK artist defends ‘Drawings Against Genocide’ show after cancellation
item.description: Matthew Collings says his work on Israeli violence against Palestinians has been intentionally misinterpreted.

item.title: Nepal in a bind as US-China drone war reaches Everest
item.description: Superpowers compete for technological foothold on world’s highest peak, pushing Nepal towards a geopolitical chessboard.

item.title: California questions FIFA’s possible violations in World Cup ticket sales
item.description: California attorney general asks FIFA to explain why the categories of some tickets were changed after being sold.

item.title: How China changed Rubio name to let him join Trump summit despite sanctions
item.description: The state secretary, who is under Chinese sanctions, is on a state visit to Beijing under the name 'Marco Lu'.

item.title: Philippine politician wanted by ICC flees Senate
item.description: Senate president says Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa 'no longer in the building' where he had taken refuge.

item.title: What is at stake as Fatah holds its 8th general congress?
item.description: Delayed for years, the event has been described as a 'quiet battle' for loyalty, succession - and the movement's future.

item.title: Why are World Cup tickets so expensive?
item.description: The 2026 World Cup is not only the biggest World Cup in history. It’s the most expensive.

item.title: Iran war day 76: Vance says progress made in talks; Israel pounds Lebanon
item.description: US Vice President Vance says progress is being made in talks with Iran as President Trump begins his trip to China.

item.title: Trump in China for talks with Xi Jinping
item.description: Trump visits Beijing for high-stakes summit with Xi as Iran, trade and Taiwan strain US-China ties.
